reviews.review_msg: Broc is a great lecturer and I highly recommend taking a class with him. His reading list was amazing and I loved all the books. He has a funny and outgoing personality that makes the lecture so enjoyable. My favourite English professor so far!

Broc was very interesting and engaging. I only took this class for the W requirement and thought it would be a grind since I've never liked English but I genuinely enjoyed this class. Study the big themes talked about in class and you will be good for the final.

AMAZING PROFESSOR. I typically don't like English, however, Broc made me look foward to every lecture. He keeps the students engaged, and he genuinely cares about his students, and humanity in general! He is also can start a class making the entire class burst into laughter over a semi-inapppropriate comment, and end it in deep thought.

Cool professor. His take on things and explanations of things can be a little bit vague, but he wants you to put the pieces together. There will be no spoon-feeding, but all of the texts we read were well-related and all really great.  The assignments aren't long or research papers, but they do require you to mull over ideas and stew them for a whi

Broc is hilarious, honestly 18th century lit does not sound like a particularly riveting class, and the readings are long, difficult, and numerous but I live for his lectures. The only thing to keep in mind when taking English 205 is that attendance is compulsory both in lecture and tutorial- so not a class for someone who likes to ditch.

I took hum340 humanities and philosophy and dropped it after 2 clasaes cus it was too hard for me... But he seems really really friendly and he is open to different opinions and really listens and tries to understand each students opinions
really bad teacher, he doesn't explain what he wants or mark fairly by any means. I would not recommend taking a class with him.

Broc is a very knowledagble and passionate professor when it comes to poetry! One of my favourites at SFU. He really wants his students to do well, so if I didn't understand something he tried his best to clarify it. He marks essays fairly if you follow assignment criteria (and revisions). I would definitely take another English class with him!!

Powerful command over the English language, lovely to listen to. Even better that he has a great sense of humour, and genuinely cares for the well-being of his students. You don't come across a professor like this very often. One of the best profs out there for sure.

Quizzes were  unecessarily hard. The averages were always 310.  Available at office hours physically but not there to help. Always answers back with a vague question. Underlines issues with your paper but when you ask him to explain it, he will say it is not his job to write your paper. He made poetry boring and textbook was uneccessary.

Great prof! One of my favorites at SFU and is genuinely interested in the subject and seeing his students succeed. He's very glad to help as well fun and keeps his classes/seminars interesting. I've taken more than once course with him and each time I felt I was really learning something, not just fulfilling a requirement.

This prof is officially my second fav prof at SFU. He was incredibly funny, clear in what he wanted and was very nice! The way he taught the class was very helpful and informative; you have to attend and pay attention (in regards to person below me)but put in the effort and he will recognize your effort. Favourite class so far. Loved it.

Professor Rossell is one of the best English instructors. While Metrics and Prosody is not always an easy topic to learn, his easygoing style of teaching made it possible. It was difficult at times to see him in office hours only because the lineup to his office was down the hall!
Engaging, and committed to helping his students learn. Very personable, and knows how to reach students. Made me want to start writing again, inside and outside of the classroom.
